title: RADIO-1: packet connect is unsafe on-air — unbounded airtime, abort can't halt TX, pre-connect DISC
description: INCIDENT 2026-05-22: a packet dial over the new RFCOMM socket ran ~110s of repeated SABM keying with no reachable abort; operator had to power OFF the radio to stop it. This is a RADIO-1 violation. Do NOT dial packet (any transport) until ALL of the below land. Found via Codex adrev (dev/adversarial/2026-05-22-ax25-socket-rx-runaway-codex.md, gitignored). FIX BUNDLE: (1) Bounded airtime — split the connect SABM-retry policy from data N2; one SABM + at most one retry + a HARD total-elapsed cap (~20-30s) regardless of n2_retries. (2) Abort before every transmit — AbortableByteLink::write must check the flag (currently only read() does, link.rs:80); connect() must check abort before push_kiss_params and before EACH SABM (currently TXes at top of each attempt); never key after Cancel. (3) No pre-connect DISC — connect() builds Ax25Stream{closed:false} before the link is up, so a failed dial (DM/timeout/abort) drops the stream -> Drop -> disconnect() -> sends DISC + waits T1 (datalink.rs:714). Only arm teardown after a UA is received. (4) REVERT the T1 floor (config.rs into_params MIN_RF_T1_MS, added this session) — it tripled worst-case airtime (3s->10s per retransmit) and was the wrong lever. (5) Build/test on task-amd-main-ui (has the abort + resize UI); main lacks them. Blocks tuxlink-uhc and tuxlink-nx2.

title: Pi-side Bluetooth ACL Page-Timeout: Pi can't connect to UV-Pro (EHOSTDOWN) — blocks all on-Pi radio work
description: Pandora's BlueZ can no longer open an ACL to the UV-Pro (38:D2:00:01:55:5C). Every connect path fails: l2ping/raw-RFCOMM-socket/bluetoothctl = 'Host is down'/EHOSTDOWN; GUI = 'no usable services' (expected for bare SPP); sdptool returns nothing. DECISIVE: btmon shows 'Connect Complete -> Status: Page Timeout (0x04)' — the radio doesn't answer the Pi's BR/EDR page (below SPP/RFCOMM/bond). Pair-OK/connect-fail split (re-pair SUCCEEDS, connect page-times-out) points at Pi-side page params/timing. wlan0 down so coexistence ruled out. Tried+failed: restart bluetoothd, hciconfig reset, rfkill cycle, full hci_uart_bcm unbind/rebind+firmware-reload, Codex#1 btmgmt le-off cycle (btmgmt hangs interactively - le-off latch unconfirmed). Radio is known-good (Winlink Express + WoAD use it as raw-KISS BT TNC). Codex consulted (dev/adversarial/2026-05-23-bt-rfcomm-unreachable-codex.md). RESUME: handoff 2026-05-23-gully-marsh-maple-rf-harness-bt-pagetimeout.md §2 — Codex#2 (clear /var/lib/bluetooth device+cache dirs + re-pair), fresh inquiry before connect, else reboot. Blocks tuxlink-4ef on-air test + rfcomm-rx-dump transport test.

title: Close the abort-before-write TOCTOU: disarm the serial/Bluetooth transport on abort (not just flag-gate)
description: Codex adrev of tuxlink-2y4 (commit ea8ac94, 2026-05-22, agent marsh-hemlock-lichen) found a check-then-write race in AbortableByteLink::write (link.rs:80): the abort flag is loaded, THEN inner.write runs — if abort is set in that window, one in-flight KISS frame (a ~20-byte SABM) still keys the radio. BOUNDED, not a runaway: datalink::connect is hard-capped at <=2 SABMs + connect_timeout, so the worst case is ONE extra short SABM if Cancel lands in a sub-microsecond window; the 110s runaway (the original RADIO-1 incident) is fixed. Two related findings: (a) TCP-KISS (link.rs:139) is not wrapped in AbortableByteLink at all — its abort is via socket shutdown, same TOCTOU class (lower concern: TCP is loopback/Dire Wolf). (b) connect_timeout is honored only within ByteLink::read's poll granularity (~200ms via LINK_POLL_TIMEOUT) — fine for all current transports (TCP/serial/RfcommSocket all set read+write timeouts), theoretical for a no-timeout link. FIX: wire abort to DISARM the transport (shutdown the RfcommSocket fd / drop the serialport handle) so an in-flight or subsequent write FAILS at the OS layer, eliminating the race — mirror TCP's abort_handle shutdown for the socket/serial paths. TDD hardware-free; on-air verification operator-run (RADIO-1). Raw adrev: dev/adversarial/2026-05-22-ax25-radio1-safety-bundle-codex.md (gitignored, local).

title: RFCOMM socket transport: TX works but RX broken — relay reply never received (needs bounded on-air byte capture)
description: The new RFCOMM-socket transport (tuxlink-nx2) keys the radio (TX works) but connect() NEVER receives the relay's UA/DM, so it loops the full n2_retries. The OLD serialport-TTY path (connect_serial) was PROVEN to receive (the W7MOT-6 DM in the chasm-sorrel-glade on-air session). So the socket introduced a no-RX regression. Codex + main-loop agree this can't be diagnosed from source — needs raw byte evidence: add timestamped hex logging at RfcommSocket::read/write, then ONE bounded+abortable on-air dial. If the socket gets 0 bytes while the relay replies -> RFCOMM/SPP control-line/service issue (or fall back to the proven TTY); if bytes arrive but don't decode -> KISS/AX.25 decode bug. NOTE: the prior 'serialport termios teardown' diagnosis that motivated the socket pivot was never proven; the Broken-pipe episodes are better explained by stale rfcomm bind / wrong channel / wrong-worktree build. Depends on the RADIO-1 safety bundle landing first (no on-air until bounded+abortable).
notes: Instrumented on bd-tuxlink-oxi/consolidate: timestamped hex RX/TX trace at RfcommSocket::read/write, opt-in via TUXLINK_RFCOMM_TRACE. Awaiting the operator's ONE bounded+abortable on-air dial (RADIO-1) — now safe to run after tuxlink-2y4 landed. Diagnostic: TX-but-no-RX => transport/SPP; RX-but-no-decode => KISS/AX.25.

title: Native client is opaque: emit the raw B2F protocol exchange to the session log (Raw output toggle)
description: Observability regression from the Pat->native cutover. Pat (wl2k-go) printed the full wire dialogue ([WL2K-...], ;PQ, ;FW, SID, ;PR, FF/FQ, session stats) to stderr, which the session log ingested (append_pat_line). The native client (winlink_backend native_connect -> telnet::connect_and_exchange) performs the real B2F exchange (handshake.rs) but only emits ~3 genericized progress() summary lines; it never tees the raw wire lines into the session log. Result: 'Raw output' shows nothing raw for native sessions -> operator cannot verify a real session happened (looks generic + too fast). Fix: tee each raw protocol line (both directions) from the native wire I/O into the durable session log + broadcast, so the Raw output toggle shows the genuine Express-style trace; keep Human as the projection. Gate with an end-to-end client test against cms-z telnet asserting the log contains the real banner + tuxlink SID + FF/FQ (NOT a mocked stream). Found 2026-05-22 by spruce-marten-cardinal; operator-directed.

description: The packet backend (P1 codec, P2 datalink state machine, P3 Winlink integration) and the P4 UI exist + unit/loopback tests pass, but NOTHING has been run end-to-end — not against a real KISS modem, not on hardware, not on-air. The operator running the app found the UI shipped as a non-functional mockup (false 'Listening' status, 127.0.0.1 leaking into USB/BT, duplicated digipeater text, no device selector, Listen entirely unwired). This session fixed those (PR #115) but the feature is still not demonstrably working. BUILD the real integration chain so a connect/listen actually completes against a real KISS modem (TCP/Dire Wolf is the most-complete transport — make it genuinely work first), with real device/transport flows. Operator directive 2026-05-22: NO mock harnesses (they get wired to prod wrong or aren't useful) — build the feature; validate by RUNNING it, not by declaring done.
notes: PROVEN end-to-end (swallow-kite-isthmus, 2026-05-22, PR #115): two real NativeBackend instances complete a full AX.25 connect (SABM/UA) + B2F message transfer over a real TCP+KISS wire — message crosses dialer-outbox -> answerer-inbox. Test: packet_two_real_peers_complete_a_connect_and_b2f_over_tcp_kiss (winlink_backend.rs). NO mocks: every layer is shipping code; only a transparent byte-relay stands in for the TNC+RF PHY. Fixed the headline bug it surfaced: the Answer(master) B2F handshake sent no '>' prompt + read with the wrong terminator -> deadlock (grounded vs wl2k-go; build_master_handshake + read_slave_handshake). Codex round then found + fixed 3 RF-real defects the relay masked: BLOCKER BufReader-vs-Ok(0) premature-EOF (BlockingB2fStream adapter), answer() not pushing KISS params, CRLF peek. 304 lib tests green. REMAINING before release-done: (1) operator app-smoke (tauri dev) to confirm UI->command wiring; (2) independent-decoder validation vs Dire Wolf/kernel-AX.25 (new follow-up issue) since peer-to-peer shares the codec; (3) hardware USB/BT (tuxlink-jvp); (4) on-air (RADIO-1, operator). Left in_progress for operator to confirm app-smoke.

title: Abort/Cancel control for an in-flight CMS connection
description: Operator: no way to cancel a connect that's slow/stalled. Need an Abort control in the dashboard (next to Connect, shown while connecting) + a backend cancel path. The connect runs in tokio::task::spawn_blocking (winlink_backend.rs); cancelling blocking socket I/O needs a shutdown handle — hold the TcpStream (or a shutdown signal) so an abort command can .shutdown(Both) to unblock the blocked connect/read, returning status to Disconnected. New cms_abort command + a frontend button bound to the Connecting state.

Commit 1163f04. Full mid-connect cancel = operator smoke (recipe in handoff).

title: Native CMS connect can stall silently (no connect timeout, no progress logging)
description: Operator hit a stall against cms-z: session log shows 'Connecting to the CMS (CmsSsl)…' then nothing. winlink/telnet.rs connect_stream uses TcpStream::connect with NO connect timeout (blocks ~75-130s on a dead endpoint); the 60s read/write timeout only applies post-connect; TLS handshake + telnet_login + run_exchange can each hang up to 60s; and there is no per-step progress logging between 'Connecting…' and the result, so any slow phase reads as a dead stall. Fix: TcpStream::connect_timeout (fail-fast, ~10-15s), per-step progress log lines (TCP connected / TLS established / secure login / proposals), and consider phase-appropriate timeouts. Relates to the abort-control + the earlier-confirmed working TLS path (so pinpoint via native_cms_probe).
notes: ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED (granite-finch-spruce, 2026-05-21): raw TCP differential — cms-z:8773(TLS)=FILTERED(15s timeout, =operator ETIMEDOUT); cms-z:8772(plaintext)=OPEN 0.07s; production server.winlink.org has BOTH 8772+8773. native_cms_probe over cms-z:8772 plaintext completes a FULL session (TCP+telnet login+B2F+MD5 secure-login+proposals). So cms-z does NOT expose TLS on 8773; commit 7c50359 set dev host=cms-z but left default transport=CmsSsl/8773. Fix scope: (1) TcpStream::connect_timeout(~15s) in winlink/telnet.rs connect_stream; (2) per-step progress logging via progress sink injected into NativeBackend at construction (NOT log_tx broadcast — that drain is dead per bootstrap.rs:392); (3) dev transport coherence — mirror probe's TUXLINK_CMS_PLAINTEXT/TUXLINK_CMS_PORT env overrides in native_connect, keep CmsSsl as production/persisted default.

title: PatProcess::spawn hardening: enforce announce timeout (no hang) + drain/null Pat stdout (no deadlock)
description: Surfaced by Codex adrev R1 on tuxlink-22l (2026-05-20). Two pre-existing robustness bugs in src-tauri/src/pat_process.rs::spawn: (#7) the 10s announce deadline is NOT actually enforced — it uses blocking BufReader::read_line, so if Pat stays alive but emits no newline the read blocks forever despite the Instant deadline check (the deadline is only re-checked between lines). Fix: read stderr via a timeout thread / recv_timeout / non-blocking poll so the deadline is real. (#18) Pat stdout is piped (Stdio::piped()) but never read, so a chatty Pat can fill the OS pipe buffer and block the child. Fix: set stdout to Stdio::null() (or drain it). tuxlink-22l's bootstrap depends on a spawn that cannot hang.
notes: Codex pqg adrev R1 #2 (2026-05-20) ALSO flagged a Child leak in PatProcess::spawn: on announce timeout the child is killed WITHOUT wait() (zombie), and a pid_file write failure after spawn drops Child without killing it. Fix alongside the announce-timeout/stdout-drain hardening: wrap Child in an armed guard right after cmd.spawn(), kill+wait on every error path, disarm only when returning PatProcess. VERIFY #88's spawn rewrite covers this before closing xyd.
close_reason: Delivered by PR #88 (merged 2026-05-21T12:59Z). 22l: PatBackend::spawn + app-start bootstrap. xx3: SessionLogState ring buffer (Task A). xyd: spawn hardening verified by peregrine-spruce-knoll — recv_timeout announce deadline (real, not in-loop Instant), Stdio::null stdout drain, Child kill+wait on every error path (L193/207/224) + impl Drop (L284), no bare ? between spawn and success return.

title: Tray restore broken on Wayland/wf-panel-pi: window hidden-to-tray can't be brought back
description:
Found 2026-05-20 (pika-glade-bluff) during operator review of the cbz tauri-dev run. Close-to-tray hides the main window (lib.rs CloseRequested -> window.hide() + prevent_close), but on this Pi (Wayland + wf-panel-pi) the operator cannot bring the window back from the tray — it 'persists but is not accessible'. Pre-existing Task-8 (tuxlink-rit) behavior; tray.rs is untouched by tuxlink-cbz. The handoff only verified tray->Quit, never tray->restore on this compositor.

LIKELY CAUSES (tray.rs):
1. Left-click restore relies on on_tray_icon_event TrayIconEvent::Click{Left,Up} -> show_main_window. wlroots/SNI panels (wf-panel-pi) commonly do NOT forward left-click 'Activate' to the SNI host, so left-click never fires -> nothing happens.
2. The right-click menu 'Show/Hide Window' (tray:show_hide) calls toggle_main_window, which branches on window.is_visible(). After a Wayland window.hide(), is_visible() is unreliable; if it reports stale 'visible', the toggle hides again and the window never re-shows.
3. window.hide()->show() reliability on Wayland/WebKitGTK is itself a known Tauri pain point (window may not re-map).

FIX IDEAS:
- Replace the visibility-TOGGLE menu item with an explicit, unconditional 'Show Window' that calls show() + unminimize() + set_focus() (and a separate 'Hide'), so restore never depends on is_visible(). show_main_window already does the unconditional show — route the menu through it.
- Add unminimize() to the show path.
- Don't depend on left-click activation as the only obvious restore (keep it, but the menu must be reliable).
- Verify on the actual Wayland/wf-panel-pi setup (operator-run; agent can't see the native window) AND on X11 for regression.

IMPACT: a window closed to tray can become unrecoverable except by killing/restarting the process — bad for an emcomm tool whose whole point is surviving close-to-tray mid-session. P1.
acceptance_criteria: From a hidden-to-tray state on Wayland/wf-panel-pi: right-click tray -> Show Window reliably restores + focuses the main window every time (no double-click, no inverted toggle). Restore also works after multiple hide/show cycles. Verified by the operator on the Pi's actual compositor; X11 path not regressed. Pat child process still survives close-to-tray (the feature that motivated hide-on-close).
notes: WORSE THAN RESTORE-TOGGLE (pika-glade-bluff 2026-05-20): on the operator's current Wayland/wf-panel-pi session the tray icon does NOT appear AT ALL (no SNI host -> TrayIconBuilder.build() silently no-ops). So close-to-tray (lib.rs CloseRequested -> hide()) strands the window: process stays alive (confirmed target/debug/tuxlink running hidden) with NO GUI path back. Only recovery is pkill + relaunch. COMPOUNDING: tauri-plugin-window-state (lib.rs:40, default flags) persists VISIBILITY for ALL windows incl. main -> a clean exit while hidden would save visible=false and the NEXT launch could start hidden too (not yet triggered: no .window-state.json saved yet). REQUIRED FIX must guarantee recoverability, e.g.: (a) minimize instead of hide on Linux (stays in compositor window list), OR (b) verify the tray actually registered before enabling hide-on-close (else fall back to minimize/real-close), OR (c) a global show-window shortcut; AND exclude StateFlags::VISIBLE from window-state for the main window so it can never restore hidden. Title broadened: not just 'restore broken' but 'tray may be absent -> close strands window'. Still P1 (arguably higher — strands an emcomm app).

title: Keyring integration tests write to the operator's REAL system keyring (XDG_DATA_HOME/HOME not isolated)
description: ROOT CAUSE of the 2026-05-20 keyring breakage. tuxlink's keyring integration tests call entry.set_password() against the real Secret Service: wizard_integration_test.rs:92/209 ('should write to real keyring') and wizard_persist_cms_test.rs:171. The XdgGuard (wizard_integration_test.rs:47-53) isolates ONLY XDG_CONFIG_HOME. gnome-keyring stores under XDG_DATA_HOME / HOME (~/.local/share/keyrings/), which is NOT isolated; dbus-run-session isolates only the D-Bus session bus, not the on-disk keyring files. So 'isolated' keyring tests AND any setup recipe (e.g. SSH_AUTH_SOCK=/run/user/1000/keyring/ssh with a typed password) reach straight into the operator's REAL, cross-project-shared (geographica also uses secret-tool against this same login keyring) system keyring. On 2026-05-20 a manual setup one-liner (bash history line 1809) re-keyed the operator's login keyring with an unrecoverable password, breaking secret access for BOTH tuxlink and geographica until a reset. FIX: (1) keyring tests MUST set a throwaway HOME (or XDG_DATA_HOME) so gnome-keyring uses a temp keyring dir, IN ADDITION to dbus-run-session; assert the test never resolves to ~/.local/share/keyrings. (2) Provide a documented safe headless-keyring-test recipe (ephemeral HOME + dbus-run-session + gnome-keyring-daemon --unlock with EMPTY password inside the sandbox). (3) Purge any setup guidance that runs --unlock against the real keyring. Cross-project hazard; bites the shared-CI/dev-environment transfer goal.

title: Define WinlinkBackend trait — v0.5 prep Step 1 (Pat greenfield refactor)
description:
Operator decision 2026-05-18 (Codex + Cameron convergence): tuxlink moves off Pat to native Rust at v0.5 via staged replacement (not flag-day). Step 1 is the architectural boundary — define a WinlinkBackend trait that decouples tuxlink's UI/config layer from the Pat HTTP sidecar so future native + Pat implementations can co-exist behind one interface.

Trait surface (from current Pat usage in src-tauri/src/pat_process.rs + pat-client integration):
- list_messages(folder: Folder) -> Vec<MessageMeta>
- read_message(id: MessageId) -> Message
- send_message(msg: OutboundMessage) -> Result<MessageId>
- connect(transport: TransportConfig) -> Result<SessionHandle>
- disconnect(session: SessionHandle) -> Result<()>
- status() -> BackendStatus
- stream_log() -> impl Stream<Item = LogLine>

Two conceptual impls:
1. PatBackend — wraps current HTTP-to-Pat-sidecar. Lands in this issue's PR.
2. NativeBackend — placeholder/stub in this issue; fleshed out in Steps 3-10.

Discipline: this IS a hard-to-undo architectural decision (the trait shape constrains every future backend implementation), so build-robust-features applies — BUT TIGHTLY SCOPED per the operator's 2026-05-18 framing:
- Brainstorm: 1 hour max (concept only — error model, async vs sync, session-handle ownership, log-stream cancellation)
- Spec: trait definition + behavior contract + 5-10 test cases (NOT 24+)
- >=1 cross-provider adrev round (NOT 5 — Codex review on the trait surface only)
- Plan: trivial (trait file + stub NativeBackend + PatBackend wrapper around current pat-client code)
- Ship as one PR

Follow-up steps (each gets its own bd issue when this one merges):
2. Freeze Pat backend as reference (PatBackend implements the trait)
3. Build native mailbox + B2F parser/writer with golden fixtures from Pat / wl2k-go
4. Build B2F session state machine over generic async IO
5. Build CMS telnet client + fake CMS harness
6. Pat-vs-native parity tests over same fixtures
7. Operator-only live CMS smoke gate
8. AX.25/KISS layer (KISS = protocol compliance per memory feedback_ai_amateur_radio_reliability)
9. Native VARA as separate milestone (clean-room: reverse-engineering writeups read as inverse spec, NOT adoption guide)
10. Default native after parity+soak; remove Pat sidecar after one fallback release

Memories to read before starting:
- feedback_discipline_triage_rule (this IS architectural; pipeline applies, but tightly scoped)
- feedback_clean_sheet_concepts_only
- feedback_ai_amateur_radio_reliability
- project_v05_modem_design_posture
- project_g90_vara_standard_works_firsthand
- feedback_no_atomic_decisions_to_operator

Replaces nothing in v0.0.1 — this is purely v0.5 prep. v0.0.1 continues to ship the Pat sidecar via the cred-handling fork (PR-A merged + PR-B merged); the trait wraps that existing surface without disturbing the shipped wizard or PatProcess flow.

description:

title: ADR 0011: Fork Pat for tuxlink (cred-handling + ongoing refactor)
description:
Architectural decision triggered 2026-05-18 during Task 6 brainstorm. Cameron rejected continuing to depend on upstream Pat as-is because Pat persists WL2K passwords to its config.json in plaintext, which is a non-negotiable defect for a production system. Bandaging the symptom at each tuxlink call site (env-var workarounds, temp-file mitigations) is bandaid spiraling.

Decision (Cameron, inline): FORK Pat as a tuxlink-owned variant. Refactor cred-handling to use OS keyring as the first patch. Continue refactoring other Pat limitations as discovered, using the full build-robust-features pipeline per refactor. Open to upstream contributions where the fork's changes are accepted upstream.

This ADR captures: context (the bandaid pattern), decision (fork), alternatives considered (continue-as-is / Rust rewrite / hybrid), consequences (Go codebase to maintain, fork-sync discipline, v0.0.1 scope expansion to include fork-setup + cred refactor + plan amendments for affected tasks 5/6/9), and the transition plan.

Light pipeline: decision is made; ADR is a write-up + Codex round + PR. Full build-robust-features pipeline NOT required for the ADR itself (it's documentation of a made decision). Subsequent tasks (fork-setup, cred-refactor, plan amendments) DO use the full pipeline.

title: Bug: get_tuxlink_sessions.py disagrees with block-main-checkout-race.sh on live-session detection
description:
Per towhee-wren-aspen's 2026-05-18 reviewer response (dev/incidents/2026-05-18-main-checkout-race-hook-loop-reviewer-response.md, reviewer Action 3) — the script and the hook read the same .git/session-leases/*.json files with the same 30-min default cutoff, but in the 2026-05-18 incident the script reported 'No live tuxlink sessions in this repo' while the hook was treating a 4-minutes-ago lease as live. They should agree by construction; they don't. This is a port-quality bug independent of the CLAUDE.md framing fix in PR #39 — even after the framing fix, a script that reports 'no live sessions' while the hook denies will continue to confuse future agents who consult it for legitimate informational purposes (e.g., 'is anyone else working in this repo right now?').

Reviewer's likely causes, in priority order:

1. **Timestamp parse failure (most likely).** The hook writes lastSeenUtc using bash's date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%6NZ (6-digit microseconds + trailing Z). Python's datetime.fromisoformat is stricter than people expect about fractional-second precision and trailing-Z handling. The script's parse_iso_utc returns None on parse failure, and on None the lease is silently skipped — causing under-reporting without any error message.

   Reproduce:
       ts=$(jq -r .lastSeenUtc .git/session-leases/<any-session-id>.json)
       python3 -c "
       import sys
       sys.path.insert(0, '.claude/scripts')
       from get_tuxlink_sessions import parse_iso_utc
       print(repr(parse_iso_utc('$ts')))
       "

   If None: that's the bug. Fix: align format on both sides (widen parse_iso_utc OR constrain the hook).

2. **Lease dir path mismatch.** The hook resolves the lease dir via git rev-parse --git-common-dir (= .git/session-leases/). The script resolves via Path(__file__).resolve().parent / '..' / '..' / '.claude' / 'session-leases'. If these resolve to different paths, the script reads an empty/stale dir and reports 'no sessions.'

   Reproduce: print both dirs from a test harness; compare.

3. **File-extension/glob mismatch.** Less likely. The script's sorted(lease_dir.glob('*.json')) should pick up every *.json file. If the hook is writing to a dir without .json extension, the script silently skips.

Priority: P1 (blocks future port-quality regressions of same shape; cheap fix; safety-stack relevance). Should land as its own atomic PR after diagnosis confirms which cause applies. No dependencies on PRs #37/#38/#39/#40.

title: Config-read resilience: a divergent packet.link variant (or unknown field) bricks app-open instead of degrading
description: A parallel branch (tuxlink-nx2, uhc/ax25-tx-timing) added KissLinkConfig::Bluetooth{mac}; its build wrote packet.link={"Bluetooth":...} to the SHARED ~/.config/tuxlink/config.json. Builds without that variant (main, #124/tuxlink-3pb) hard-fail on open: KissLinkConfig is externally-tagged (unknown variant rejected, no serde(other) fallback) and Config is deny_unknown_fields. read_config() returns Err and the app bricks. NOTE: read_config's documented wizard consumers (.ok() -> fresh wizard) degrade gracefully, but SOME startup path propagates the error as fatal — that propagating consumer is the fix locus (degrade to None/wizard, don't brick). OWNER: the nx2/Bluetooth work should ship read-tolerance so merging it + version-skew don't brick configs; coordinate (bd dep) rather than two sessions editing config.rs/link.rs at once. DEV HAZARD (generalizable): concurrent branch builds sharing one config + deny_unknown_fields = cross-contamination bricking (config-file cousin of the worktree :1420 collision). Mitigations: graceful read degradation on schema divergence AND/OR per-worktree TUXLINK_CONFIG_DIR for in-dev builds. Found during #124 (tuxlink-3pb) browser smoke 2026-05-22.

title: AX.25 Path::encode hardcodes C/R=command for ALL frames — responses (UA/RR-as-response) mis-framed per v2.2
description: frame.rs Path::encode (~line 224) always sets dest C-bit=1 / src C-bit=0 with comment 'cr=true for a command frame; refined in P2' — never refined. CORRECT for commands (SABM/DISC/I-frames), but WRONG for RESPONSES: a UA sent when ANSWERING an inbound call, and RR/RNR/REJ sent as responses to a poll, must per AX.25 v2.2 be dest C-bit=0 / src C-bit=1. A strict v2.2 decoder (RMS gateway, BPQ, Direwolf) may reject/mis-handle our responses. This is the independent-decoder-visible class of bug that peer-to-peer + TCP-relay testing (shared codec) could never catch — surfaced now that we're on-air against a real gateway. NOT the cause of the 2026-05-22 SABM-dial DM (a SABM IS a command and is correctly framed; address shift, SSID reserved/ext bits, control 0x3F, KISS port-0 wrap, and no-FCS-for-KISS all verified spec-correct). Fix: thread command/response through Path::encode (or Frame::encode) so response frames set the C/R bits correctly. Most impactful on the LISTEN/answer path (tuxlink-as-master sends UA) + sustained exchanges (RR acks).

title: Packet panel: transport segment resets to USB Serial instead of persisting last-selected (Bluetooth)
description: On-air 2026-05-22: the packet connect panel's transport segment 'constantly resets to USB Serial' instead of staying on Bluetooth (the last-selected mode). Likely cause (verify, do not assume): USB and Bluetooth BOTH map to linkKind:'Serial' (per spec/code 'USB or Bluetooth-RFCOMM both use kind:serial'), so when the panel re-derives the segment from config on reload/poll it can't distinguish BT from USB and defaults to USB. Fix: persist/derive the BT-vs-USB segment distinctly (stored sub-kind, or infer from device path /dev/rfcommN vs /dev/ttyUSB*). Frontend src/packet/PacketConnectionPanel.tsx. Related: the bound /dev/rfcomm0 also did NOT appear in the BT device picker (packet_list_serial_devices discovery gap) — operator had to type the path.

title: Packet AX.25: radio double-keys on connect; configured T1/TXdelay/KISS params not respected
description: On-air 2026-05-22 (UV-Pro, ch5, dial W7MOT-6): the connect caused the radio to key TWICE in very fast succession (operator: 'not good'), and the operator (RF-expert) reports the default AX.25 parameters (T1/TXdelay/persistence/slot) are NOT being honored. INVESTIGATE (do not assume a cause): (1) config->Ax25Params mapping (PacketConfig into_params) — are the configured T1/timing values actually used, or defaults? (2) kiss_param frame correctness — the KISS command nibble MUST be 1-6 (config: TXDELAY=1/P=2/SlotTime=3/...); if any is 0 (data) it would TX the payload and cause spurious keying. (3) connect() T1 retransmit loop (datalink.rs) — a too-short T1 vs gateway RF latency fires a 2nd SABM before the 1st response arrives. The connect reached the gateway (DM back) so the transport works; this is RF-politeness/timing. Needs careful look, not a session-end guess. Evidence: session log 01:41:52 connect -> 01:41:59 DM.
notes: SUPERSEDED-IN-PART by tuxlink-2y4: the T1 floor (PR #123) is to be REVERTED; the real fix (bounded airtime + abort + no pre-connect DISC) is 2y4. The double-key was one symptom of a broader unsafe connect envelope.

title: ax25: clean Stop for a serial/Bluetooth packet listen (abort over serialport)
description: packet Listen Stop works over TCP (cms_abort shuts the cloned TcpStream -> recv_frame ConnectionAborted -> answer() unwinds). For a USB/BT serial KISS TNC, connect_link_with_abort returns None (no try_clone equivalent for a serialport handle), so abort() is a no-op on the link and a waiting answer() can't be stopped except by disconnecting the TNC. Add a serial abort mechanism: e.g. a stop-flag the answer poll loop checks, or a serialport break/close handle. Built 2026-05-22 on bd-tuxlink-7fr/ax25-packet.
notes: BUILT + GATED (chasm-sorrel-glade, 2026-05-22, commit 12486ff on bd-tuxlink-nj1/serial-stop, PR #119). Serial/BT packet Listen Stop now works: AbortableByteLink (link.rs) wraps the serial link and returns ConnectionAborted once the backend's existing 'aborting' flag is set; recv_frame already maps that to an abort, so answer()/connect() unwind on the next poll (bounded by the 200ms serial read timeout). connect_link_with_abort takes the abort Arc<AtomicBool>; TCP keeps its immediate socket-shutdown fast path (flag unused there). native_packet_connect threads the existing aborting flag in (no new state). Hardware-free tests: AbortableByteLink flag->ConnectionAborted read; answer() unwinds with ConnectionAborted on an aborting link. cargo --lib 311 ok. Left IN_PROGRESS pending OPERATOR on-air verification (a real Stop against a serial/BT TNC e.g. UV-Pro /dev/rfcomm0 — RADIO-1).

title: Connect-path hardening: bounded DNS, total connect deadline, abort-label accuracy (Codex adrev follow-ups)
description:
Deferred Medium/Low findings from the 2026-05-21 Codex adversarial review of the connect cluster (gqo+9z2). The two High findings (single-flight #1, abort TOCTOU #2) + cleanup #7 were fixed in the cluster before merge; these remain:

#3 (Med) winlink/telnet.rs: to_socket_addrs() DNS resolution is UNBOUNDED before the bounded connect_timeout. A blackholed/hung resolver reintroduces the stall gqo fixed (no socket registered yet, so cms_abort can't unblock it). Fix needs a DNS-with-timeout (threaded resolve + timeout, or async resolver). Low real-world impact now: cms-z + server.winlink.org are single-A and resolve in ~0.07s.

#4 (Med) winlink/telnet.rs connect_with_timeout: CONNECT_TIMEOUT is per-address, not a total deadline. A host with N blackholed A/AAAA records can block up to N*15s. Fix: pass a total deadline; per-addr remaining = deadline - elapsed. Low impact now (current hosts single-address), but IPv6 AAAA could add addresses.

#5 (Med) winlink_backend.rs abort_aware_outcome: maps ANY error to Cancelled when aborting is set. If a real (pre-abort) failure surfaces just as the operator clicks Abort, it's mislabeled 'CMS connection aborted.' instead of the true reason. Cosmetic but can mask a real error. Fix: track whether abort actually shut down THIS connect's socket (epoch), or only map to Cancelled when a socket was actually torn down.

#6 (Low) winlink/telnet.rs connect_with_timeout: returns only the LAST address error. If addr1 = ConnectionRefused and addr2 = TimedOut, the user sees only TimedOut, losing the more actionable earlier failure. Diagnostic-only.

Raw transcript: dev/adversarial/2026-05-21-connect-cluster-codex.md (local-only, gitignored).
notes: Implemented #3/#4/#6 in telnet.rs (commit c612a48 on bd-tuxlink-lbg/connect-hardening). #5 implemented then REVERTED: Codex re-review (codex review --base main, gpt-5.5) flagged P2 — the only-Cancelled-on-teardown fix regresses the abort contract for pre-socket aborts (Abort during DNS/connect would surface as Error not Cancelled) and didn't fix the socket-exists race anyway. winlink_backend.rs left == main (aborting-keyed mapping is contract-correct). 166 lib green; full suite re-running pre-push. PR pending.

title: Project dashboard: pure-derived bd-first, tailnet-served, single-screen, multi-track + live-session
description: Local-only project dashboard for monitoring tuxlink while out (esp. during long autonomous/parallel agent sessions). Design brainstormed 2026-05-21 (alder-arroyo-bluff); spec at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-21-project-dashboard-design.md. Approach A: small Python collector + tiny HTTP server, server-rendered HTML, ~15s recompute cache, served on the tailnet (pandora 100.110.97.120, free port). PURE-DERIVED bd-first: zero hand-maintained progress config — state/blockers/velocity/ETAs computed live from bd + git + gh + worktree mtimes; tracks grouped by bd LABEL (one-time labeling pass; untracked issues bucket, fix-in-bd not in-config). Anti-goal = LFST dashboard drift (config + plan-markdown slug-inference). Trust model: one source per fact, show-your-work drilldown, honest uncertainty (ETA ranges + 'insufficient data'), DRIFT-detection card (merged-not-closed, stalled, unclaimed worktrees), minimal human surface. Scope: 3 tracks (v0.0.1 / native-client / RF-modem) + overall-progress timeline (real date axis, future=range bands) + live-session panel. Read-only; never transmits; systemd user service.
design: Components: collectors/{bd,git,gh,worktrees}.py -> model.py (pure, fixture-testable) -> render.py (Jinja2 self-contained HTML) + server.py (http.server, /api/state.json, cache) + settings.toml (bind/port/repo/refresh only). No DB. Timeline fill=elapsed-time (neutral); work-done shown via pip state + per-track bars. Future milestones=range bands. ETA separates throughput-bound from decision-gated work. Bind tailnet IP not 0.0.0.0. systemctl --user (no sudo). v1 lives in tools/dashboard/ in-repo; extractable later (transfer goal).

title: Bootstrap: supervise/reap in-flight Pat child on mid-spawn app quit
description: Surfaced by Codex R2 on tuxlink-22l (impl review, 2026-05-20, finding #5). The app-start bootstrap runs on a detached std::thread; during the PatBackend::spawn window the Pat child is not yet owned by managed state (BackendState). If the app quits mid-spawn (the ~1s announce window), there is no cancellation/supervisor path to reap the in-flight Pat child, so it can orphan (adrev #8 only partially addressed by PatProcess Drop, which only fires once PatProcess is constructed+dropped). Fix: store an in-flight supervisor/cancel handle in managed state and wire app-exit teardown (on_window_event / RunEvent::ExitRequested) to kill+reap any child started before backend installation. Narrow edge (recoverable via pid file), deferred from the 22l PR.

The wizard test-send runs first-run (pre-config, before the bootstrap spawns anything) so today they don't collide, but two notions of 'the Pat URL' is a latent bug + a double-spawn / mbox-dir / pid-file contention risk once both exist (adrev #13). title: Selectable color schemes + night/tactical modes (all-red full-screen, grayscale)
description:
Operator request (2026-05-20, v0.5 target). The mock-d 'mailapp-minimal' amber-on-warm-dark scheme is the right v0.0.1 default, but operators have varied preferences and field needs. Add USER-SELECTABLE color schemes.

Two night/tactical flavors are first-class requirements (not just nice-to-have):
1. ALL-RED night/tactical mode — optimized for full-screen use, designed to PRESERVE NIGHT VISION while remaining readable. NOT a flat monochrome dump: tasteful red-channel-dominant design with deliberate luminance hierarchy (deep red surfaces, brighter red text/accents) so structure stays legible at low light.
2. GRAYSCALE mode — for systems already using a hardware/software night-vision-preservation screen overlay (red gel / NVG-safe filter), where the app should be neutral grayscale so the external overlay does the color work.

ENABLING FOUNDATION: tuxlink-cbz centralized all component colors onto the --tux-* design tokens on :root. A selectable scheme is therefore an alternate token set (e.g. [data-theme='night-red'] :root-scope overrides). No per-component CSS rewrite needed — this is the payoff of the cbz token discipline.
design: APPROVED 2026-05-21 (sandbar-heron-bayou; operator approved the visual mock dev/scratch/color-schemes-mock.html). Scope: selectable PRESETS only (custom editor = later issue). Three schemes as [data-theme] token overrides of the primitive tokens in App.css :root (semantic --tux-* layer remaps automatically). DEFAULT = unchanged (no data-theme / 'default'). NIGHT-RED (data-theme=night-red): bg#0a0000 surface#160000 surface-2#210000 elevated#2b0000 border#3d0000 border-strong#5c0000 border-soft#1c0000 text#ff5b5b text-dim#b34a4a text-faint#6e2e2e accent#ff2d2d accent-2#ff6b6b accent-soft rgba(255,45,45,.14) unread-dot#ff7a7a success#ff9a9a error#ff2020 info#cc5a5a form-tag#d96666; --tux-accent-fg#160000 --tux-danger-fg#160000 --tux-danger-surface rgba(255,32,32,.16) --tux-focus-ring rgba(255,45,45,.55). GRAYSCALE (data-theme=grayscale): bg#0d0d0d surface#161616 surface-2#1f1f1f elevated#262626 border#2e2e2e border-strong#404040 border-soft#1a1a1a text#e8e8e8 text-dim#9a9a9a text-faint#5f5f5f accent#cfcfcf accent-2#e8e8e8 accent-soft rgba(207,207,207,.12) unread-dot#f0f0f0 success#c4c4c4 error#9e9e9e info#b4b4b4 form-tag#bcbcbc; --tux-accent-fg#0d0d0d --tux-danger-fg#0d0d0d --tux-danger-surface rgba(158,158,158,.18) --tux-focus-ring rgba(207,207,207,.45). DECISIONS: (1) switcher = native View>Color scheme submenu emitting menu:view:scheme:<id>; (2) persistence = localStorage 'tuxlink.colorScheme', applied pre-paint in main.tsx to avoid FOUC; (3) monochrome schemes collapse semantic hue -> state via luminance + icons, not color (kept success lighter / error vivid-darker for separation).
acceptance_criteria: Default scheme = current mock-d amber/warm-dark, unchanged. A settings control switches schemes at runtime + persists. At least: default, all-red night/tactical (full-screen, night-vision-preserving, readable, tasteful), grayscale (for external NVG-overlay use). All schemes implemented as --tux-* token overrides (no per-component color literals reintroduced). Each scheme passes the visual-companion smoke against the main surfaces.
close_reason: Implemented selectable color schemes (presets) as design-token overrides: default (unchanged) + night/tactical all-red (night-vision-preserving luminance ladder) + grayscale (for external NVG overlay). colorScheme.ts model (localStorage, pre-paint apply), App.css [data-theme] blocks, native View>Color scheme submenu + AppShell handler. TDD (8 model tests + menu manifest); gates green (frontend 321, tsc, Rust all pass); both schemes grim-smoked in real WebKitGTK — all components recolor via tokens. Custom-scheme editor deferred (later issue). Commit 5af09aa.

title: Session-log history ring buffer for session_log_snapshot replay (Task 15 follow-up)
description: Surfaced during integration-commit fix b6331de. session_log_snapshot is now implemented + registered + projects LogLineDto correctly, but returns an EMPTY Vec because no history exists to snapshot: the WinlinkBackend trait exposes only stream_log() (live BoxStream, no replay) and PatBackend uses a tokio::broadcast channel with no snapshot-readable history. Result: a late-mounting SessionLog seeds with [] and only sees live tail from mount onward; spec ~§198 wants the snapshot to seed prior session history. Fix: add a bounded ring buffer to the log mechanism (PatBackend / AppBackend) that session_log_snapshot reads. The command + registration + DTO projection are already in place (b6331de); only the buffer is missing. Only matters once a live session produces history (post tuxlink-22l).
notes:
ADREV INVERSION (Codex R1 on tuxlink-22l, 2026-05-20): the ring buffer is NOT a post-22l nicety — it is a PREREQUISITE for 22l's session-log delivery. tokio::broadcast only serves current subscribers, so Pat's startup lines drop before the drain subscribes; a durable last-N ring buffer (monotonic seq) is required so session_log_snapshot seeds + snapshot-then-tail loses nothing. Bumped P3->P2. See spec 2026-05-20-pat-spawn-bootstrap-design.md §11.1.
IMPLEMENTED as Task A of PR #88 (SessionLogState ring buffer + seq). Closes on PR #88 merge.
close_reason: Delivered by PR #88 (merged 2026-05-21T12:59Z). 22l: PatBackend::spawn + app-start bootstrap. xx3: SessionLogState ring buffer (Task A). xyd: spawn hardening verified by peregrine-spruce-knoll — recv_timeout announce deadline (real, not in-loop Instant), Stdio::null stdout drain, Child kill+wait on every error path (L193/207/224) + impl Drop (L284), no bare ? between spawn and success return.

title: Wizard keyring UX: locked login keyring on auto-login Linux triggers a confusing gnome-keyring prompt (not in-app guidance)
description: Found during M1 smoke 2026-05-20 on the dev Pi (auto-login enabled = Raspberry Pi OS default; login Secret Service collection Locked=true). pam_gnome_keyring is configured but cannot unlock the login keyring because auto-login enters no password. The wizard's first keyring write (wizard_persist_cms) then triggers gnome-keyring's OWN unlock dialog, which asks for the keyring password — operators confuse this with the sudo/login password and get rejected. The Rust side is correct (map_keyring_error -> WizardError::Locked; snapshot read before destructive set_password). Gap is operator-experience: (1) the wizard should pre-flight the Secret Service lock state and surface clear in-app guidance + a recovery path (unlock, or set the login keyring password-less for auto-unlock) instead of letting the raw gnome prompt confuse the operator; (2) docs/install.md should document the auto-login keyring caveat for Pi deployments. Bites the common emcomm deployment (auto-login Pi). Not an M1 code blocker; the keyring write itself works once the collection is unlocked.
notes: ROOT CAUSE of N7CPZ live-CMS auth failures (2026-05-21, gully-sage-heron, verified from source): Pat's reader go-keyring v0.2.8 searches ONLY the 'login' Secret Service collection (keyring_unix.go GetLoginCollection()), while the Rust keyring crate (wizard writer) creates items in the DEFAULT collection (keyring-3.6.3 secret_service.rs). On a normal desktop login==default so they interoperate (PR #75 verified there). On pandora post-keyring-incident they DIVERGED: login keyring moved aside (login.keyring.broken-20260520.bak), active default = Default_keyring. So wizard/secret-tool writes land in Default_keyring but Pat reads 'login' (absent) -> not found -> interactive prompt -> non-interactive live_cms_smoke stalls (context deadline). FIX: restore the standard setup where the login keyring IS the active unlocked default. Cred-handling implication (8zt/ADR 0011): tuxlink's CMS auth requires the cred in the LOGIN collection; the spec should state this collection constraint, and the wizard should detect/repair a broken-login / default!=login environment (this is the qn8 UX). NOT a tuxlink code bug.

title: Wizard test-send: Busy from a non-test-send mutex holder leaves window stuck in sending
description: Codex Part-97 re-round of b45f592 (P1 liveness, NOT transmission-safety — no double-TX path). The single-flight tokio::Mutex is shared by wizard_persist_cms, wizard_persist_offline, wizard_run_test_send (wizard.rs). The frontend treats ANY WizardError::Busy as a strict no-op that stays in 'sending'. If the contention came from a persist command or another window (not a concurrent test-send), no TEST_SEND_RESULT ever arrives and the window stays in 'sending' indefinitely. Mitigations: unreachable in the single-window first-run flow (persist completes before test-send), and recoverable via the always-present Skip control during sending. Fix options: track mutex holder/source so Busy-from-test-send vs Busy-from-other is distinguishable, OR add a 'sending' watchdog timeout -> failed. Deferred from M1 by orchestrator (grouse-esker-juniper, 2026-05-20).

title: Fix File → Quit native handling (post-PR-70 smoke gap)
description:
Smoke test of PR #70 (Task 7 menu bar) on 2026-05-19 surfaced one failure: clicking File → Quit (or pressing Ctrl+Q) does nothing. Root cause: wire_menu_events in src-tauri/src/menu.rs emits the menu:file:quit event to the 'menu' Tauri channel and stops. The React frontend (src/App.tsx — still Tauri-React boilerplate) has no listen('menu', ...) handler, so the event fires into dead air.

Every menu item has the same shape, but Quit is the one item users have a hard expectation about ('Quit must quit').

Fix: special-case Quit in wire_menu_events so it works natively without waiting for Tasks 9+ to wire the frontend listener. ~4 lines:

  if id == 'menu:file:quit' { app_for_handler.exit(0); }

The event still emits, so future frontend listeners (e.g., 'discard unsaved draft?' dialog in Task 14 Compose) can still observe + intercept later.

Discipline: plumbing-class per feedback_discipline_triage_rule — spec is this issue body, TDD-direct, no Codex round.
acceptance_criteria: File → Quit closes the app cleanly. Ctrl+Q closes the app cleanly. menu_test still passes (event manifest unchanged). cargo build --lib still warning-free. Operator-smoke-confirmed before merge.

title: Packet BT: in-app RFCOMM-socket transport (no rfcomm bind / no root / no shell) — mediate the OS setup jank
description: DEFERRED by operator 2026-05-22: do NOT design/build until connected-mode AX.25 (Winlink B2F) is PROVEN working on-air over the UV-Pro on Linux — don't automate setup for a transport that isn't proven. Captured idea + feasibility finding so it's not re-investigated. Problem: the current BT path makes the operator do raw OS shell (bluetoothctl pair + sudo rfcomm bind /dev/rfcommN + channel-hunt + flaky retries) — operator flagged this as 'Pat territory'; a savvy BT/Linux user nearly gave up, so the EmComm target audience would give up entirely. Key feasibility finding (verified on pandora, this session): a NON-root user CAN create an RFCOMM socket (AF_BLUETOOTH/BTPROTO_RFCOMM created OK as uid 1000) — so a native client can open the RFCOMM connection DIRECTLY as a socket (connect to MAC+channel), needing NO root, NO 'rfcomm bind', NO /dev/rfcommN node, NO channel-hunting (read channel from the radio's SDP record). The RFCOMM socket is a Read+Write byte stream that drops into the existing ByteLink trait exactly like TcpStream. This SUPERSEDES the spec section 4.1 serial-/dev/rfcommN decision FOR BLUETOOTH. Scope when un-deferred: (1) new KissLinkConfig::Bluetooth { mac, channel } opening an RFCOMM socket via the bluer crate (or raw socket); (2) in-app pairing via BlueZ D-Bus (scan->pick->pair) WITH AUTO-RETRY on org.bluez.Error.ConnectionAttemptFailed (the transient failure the operator hit); (3) read SPP channel from SDP, fallback to 1; (4) graceful fallback to guided prompts if in-app pairing hits a polkit wall. STILL-UNPROVEN before building: RFCOMM socket CONNECT to the radio (only creation verified) — prove that + the on-air connected-mode dial first. Brainstorm context this session; no spec written (operator deferred). Relates to tuxlink-jvp (current shell help-text) + tuxlink-3wh (on-air proof gate).
notes: Socket transport built (PR #123) + connect+hold proven via Python (channel resolved from SDP, held stable). BUT RX is broken on-air (tuxlink-4ef) and the connect envelope is RADIO-1-unsafe (tuxlink-2y4). NOT air-ready.

title: RMS node map layer: nearest Winlink RMS node by real GPS position (Tuxlink CMS fetch + Geographica render)
description:
# Feature — RMS node map layer: nearest Winlink RMS node by real GPS position (Tuxlink ⇄ Geographica)

**Captured:** 2026-05-22 by `hemlock-arroyo-mink` · **Status:** captured, not designed. Future feature. **A Tuxlink feature that touches both Tuxlink and Geographica in equal measure** — Tuxlink owns the CMS-side data + the nearest-node logic; Geographica owns the offline-map render. (Operator correction: Geographica does NOT poll the CMS — Tuxlink does.)

## Origin

Operator-relayed ask (quoted):

> "Has anyone figured out how to add Winlink RMS nodes as a map layer to either YAAC or Navit? I know that the pat winlink mode will show distance. But to my understanding, the distance calculation is made from the center of the grid square stored in ET-user. This is totally acceptable for HF modes, but is more important for VHF packet or Vara FM. I also have a GPS unit. So it'd be nice to pull up an offline map and see the node that is actually closest to me."

Operator's note: **"We can already do this if we have access to the WL2K RMS node list, which is a thing you can request from the CMS."**

## Value proposition

Pat computes RMS distance from the **center of the user's own grid square** (the `ET-user` entry). With a GPS unit, the operator knows their **precise** position. Using precise GPS as the distance origin — instead of grid-square-center — is:

- **Acceptable to skip for HF** (long range; grid-center error is negligible relative to skip distance).
- **Materially better for VHF packet / VARA FM** (short, near-line-of-sight range; a grid-square is ~mi across, so grid-center error can flip which node is genuinely closest/reachable).

Deliverable: pull up an **offline** map and see the RMS node **actually closest to me**, by real GPS position.

## Architecture — who owns what

**Tuxlink owns (the data + logic half):**
- Fetch the **WL2K RMS node list from the CMS** (callsign, frequency, mode, grid square / position, possibly hours/service). Operator confirms this list is requestable from the CMS.
- Compute nearest-node distance from the operator's **precise GPS position** (not grid-center) to each node.
- Leverages the **position subsystem just landed in `tuxlink-686` / PR #109** (Maidenhead ↔ lat/lon conversion, distance) — that's the enabling piece. This feature should take a `bd dep` on tuxlink-686.

**Geographica owns (the render half):**
- Offline base map + render the RMS node set as a **map layer / POI overlay**.
- Highlight the nearest node; show the operator's GPS position.

The cross-project boundary: Tuxlink produces a node dataset (+ the operator's position); Geographica consumes it as a layer. When the Geographica half is built, track it in Geographica's own tracker with a dep back to this issue (sibling repos run from their own session roots).

## Correctness / posture notes

- **GPS precision:** the project's precision-reduction default (broadcast position → 4-char Maidenhead by default; opt-in to higher precision) governs **what you transmit**. This nearest-node calc is **local-only** — using **full GPS precision** for it is correct and does NOT conflict with that posture. A future implementer must not down-reduce precision for the local distance calc.
- **Node-position precision:** the win comes from precise *user* position. The *nodes'* positions may themselves only be grid squares in the CMS list — acceptable (nodes are fixed; the dominant error source the operator is complaining about is the user-side grid-center origin).

## Open items to verify when picked up

- **Exact CMS request mechanism** for the RMS node list, and the fields it returns (does it carry precise lat/lon, or grid square only?). Verify against Winlink docs — do not assume (ham-radio specifics are a known-unreliable area).
- **Does `tuxlink-pat` already expose RMS-list retrieval?** Upstream Pat has RMS-list functionality (`pat` gateway/`rmslist`-class features); the fork may already provide the fetch, reducing this to parse + compute + hand-off. Verify in the fork.
- **List freshness / caching** for true offline use (cache last-fetched list on disk; refresh when online).
- **Geographica ingestion format** for a custom POI/node layer (GeoJSON? waypoint set?).
- **Prior art (the quoted open question):** has anyone done RMS-node map layers in **YAAC** or **Navit**? Research when picked up — may inform the layer format and UX.

The dev/scratch copy is a local convenience; the tracked doc is canonical. title: Frequency/rig control plane: single source of truth tying station + frequency (research)
description: v0.1 ships packet 'Frequency' as a NOTE-ONLY field (operator tunes the radio manually). Future vision (operator Cameron, 2026-05-22 AX.25 brainstorm): a single source-of-truth control plane that binds the target station + frequency + mode together for an RF session, so the client and radio cannot mismatch. A client/radio frequency mismatch can cause real harmful interference (transmitting on the wrong freq); a unified control surface focuses operator attention and prevents that class of error. Cameron has done freq-coordinated calling manually and wants the tool to own it eventually. SCOPE OF RESEARCH (heavy): rig-control protocols (Hamlib/rigctld, CAT per-radio differences incl. the G90), how to set freq/mode safely from tuxlink, interlocks/confirmation to prevent accidental wrong-freq TX, and how channel definitions (callsign+freq+mode, per the Winlink channel-data model) map to a tuxlink 'channel/control-plane' concept. Deliverable of the research pass: a feasibility + design note and a go/no-go. Depends on the v0.1 packet feature landing first.

title: Attachment shrink-to-fit: operator-controlled source-byte reducer for low-bandwidth sends (post-v0.0.1)
description:
# Feasibility note — Attachment shrink-to-fit / source-byte reducer (post-v0.0.1)

**Captured:** 2026-05-21 by `hemlock-arroyo-mink` · **Status:** research captured, NOT designed. Revisit when attachments are prioritized (clearly post-v0.0.1 — attachments are list-add-only today).

**Origin:** Operator question — sending PDFs/images over 1200-baud packet and VARA HF is bandwidth-limited; would an attachment compressor help, and could a Pi do it? Research pass run; this note is the durable capture so the analysis isn't lost.

---

## The reframe that governs everything

**Winlink already compresses every attachment losslessly in transit.** The B2 Forwarding Protocol (B2F) bundles the body + all attachments (up to 5 messages) and runs them through **LZHUF** (the FBB B1 compressor) before transmission. It deliberately runs the modem in "MODE 0" — no native modem compression — so VARA/Pactor/packet add nothing on top in the Winlink path.

**Consequence:** "beat .zip" is the wrong frame. Zip and LZHUF are both general-purpose lossless archivers. Running one over an already-entropy-maximized JPEG/PNG/PDF buys ~5% or worse and can inflate it with container overhead. Re-zipping before send is near-useless. **The only lever that helps is reducing SOURCE bytes before B2F sees them: resize, lossy re-encode, transcode to a denser codec, or substitute a leaner format.**

## Binding constraint — recipient decodability

The typical Winlink recipient runs **Winlink Express on Windows** (or Pat, or a webmail gateway). The Winlink Express picture editor handles only **jpeg, gif, png, bmp, tiff**. Modern dense codecs (**WebP / AVIF / JPEG XL / HEIC**) are NOT reliably decodable by a stock Windows recipient and are effectively non-deliverable for general recipients.

**Universally-safe delivery formats:** JPEG, PNG, GIF, BMP, TIFF (images); PDF, plain text / CSV / RTF (documents). JPEG is the safest lossy-photo target.

This is why **jpegli** is the sweet spot for photos: modern-encoder-class savings (~35% smaller than libjpeg at equal quality) while the output is still a *normal JPEG* any ancient decoder opens.

---

## The four questions, answered

### 1. Can we do meaningfully better than .zip?
Yes, by a wide margin — but not as a better archiver. Zip barely helps (the wire already runs LZHUF). The wins come from source reduction:

| Lever | Saving vs. original | Recipient-safe? |
|---|---|---|
| Resize + re-encode JPEG (target-KB) | 5–20× (dominant for photos) | yes (JPEG) |
| jpegli / mozjpeg encoder | extra ~35% at equal quality, output still JPEG | yes |
| WebP / AVIF / JPEG XL | 25–55% smaller than JPEG | mostly NO |
| PDF DPI downsample (Ghostscript `/screen`) | MB → tens of KB for scans | yes (still PDF) |
| Format substitution (XLSX→CSV, DOCX→RTF/TXT) | 5×+ | yes |
| JBIG2 / CCITT-G4 / DjVu (scanned text) | 10–50× | DjVu no; CCITT-G4-in-PDF/TIFF yes |

### 2. Can the Pi do it without falling over?
Yes, with codec discipline. The risk is one specific codec, not the workload.
- **Use libvips** for resize/decode/encode — streams in tiles, ~constant small RAM regardless of image dimensions. Single most important "don't take the Pi down" choice.
- **Avoid libaom AVIF as default** (~47× slower than WebP; ~20 s/MP at slow presets — this is the CPU hog). **Avoid guetzli entirely** (~90 s, ~300 MB RAM for one 1-MP image).
- Fast + safe on ARM: jpegli/mozjpeg (~300 ms/MP), cwebp (near-JPEG speed), Ghostscript for PDFs.
- A single interactive "shrink this attachment" on a Pi 5 with libvips + jpegli finishes sub-second for a typical photo. Cap encoder threads, one attachment at a time.

### 3. How out of scope is auto-".jpg make it fit"?
Not wildly — it's the most-precedented, smallest viable slice. **Winlink Express already ships exactly this**: a Resize dialog that takes a target KB and re-encodes to hit it (documented example: 83.5 KB → 30.2 KB at target → 7.0 KB after tight crop). The algorithm is a bounded **binary search over (scale factor, JPEG quality)** to land just under a target KB, with a live before/after preview. Scope grows only if "fit under the limit" expands beyond photos (see scope tiers).

### 4. Clever methods worth knowing
- **Format substitution is the community's #1 document trick** (EmComm training: "save XLSX→CSV cuts size 5×+, DOCX→RTF/TXT; do NOT attach the original spreadsheet"). An "attachment lint" advisor could beat any codec for office docs.
- **Winlink already does template/delta encoding for forms** — ICS-213/Radiogram are tiny XML field-data attachments rendered against a local template library at the receiver, not rendered images. Generalizable lever for forms-heavy EmComm: send field values + template ID. Biggest conceptual win for the EmComm domain.
- **The best compression is often not sending the image** — OCR a scanned text page to plain text (KB vs. tens-of-KB); send "2-story structure fire, NE corner, ~30% involved" as text rather than a photo. Matches EmComm doctrine.
- Progressive/thumbnail-first transmission (app convention, not protocol — Winlink is store-and-forward). Vector-tracing line art (potrace→SVG, but SVG only recipient-safe wrapped in PDF).

## Scope tiers (for when this is revisited)
1. **Photo shrink-to-fit only** — operator dialog, target-KB, libvips+jpegli binary search, before/after preview. Smallest, highest-precedent, lowest integrity risk. Delivers most day-to-day value.
2. **Photos + documents** — adds Ghostscript PDF DPI-downsample + "leaner format" nudges. Medium. Covers bulk of real EmComm attachment traffic.
3. **Full content-adaptive system** — classifier routes photo / line-art / scanned-doc to the right codec; OCR-to-text; possibly template/delta for forms. Large, research-flavored, biggest payoff and biggest integrity-risk surface.

## Integrity traps to manage
- Lossy JBIG2 symbol-substitution can silently swap digits (the Xerox scanning bug) — for EmComm where numbers matter, use lossless JBIG2 / CCITT-G4 only.
- OCR errors on poor scans; over-aggressive lossy recompression of critical content.
- Keep the operator in the loop (matches the project's operator-controlled framing) — never silently transform an EmComm-critical attachment.

## Provenance / honesty flags
- Q1, Q2, and the prior-art in Q4 are anchored on Winlink **primary docs** read directly (B2F spec, Winlink Express picture-editor slides, EmComm-Training exercise PDFs).
- Soft spots, not overstated: (a) one secondary note questioned whether Winlink also disables VARA's internal Huffman — does not change the LZHUF conclusion; (b) there is NO canonical ham "always 320px / quality 40" rule — the real convention is target-KB-driven, which Winlink Express confirms.
- Operator (Cameron) has operated VARA HF on a G90 firsthand — his sense of real-world throughput floors overrides any specific bps figure from a web source.

## Key sources
- Winlink B2F protocol spec: https://winlink.org/B2F
- LZHUF compression source: https://github.com/ARSFI/Winlink-Compression
- RMS Express picture editing (target-KB resize, supported formats): https://philsherrod.com/Winlink/RMS_Express_Picture_Editing.pdf
- EmComm-Training, file attachments / format substitution: https://emcomm-training.org/Winlink-Thursday__File-Attachments-rev.1.1.pdf
- Winlink FAQ (120 KB cap): https://winlink.org/sites/default/files/download/wl2k_faq_5.pdf
- jpegli (standard-JPEG-compatible, ~35% smaller): https://opensource.googleblog.com/2024/04/introducing-jpegli-new-jpeg-coding-library.html
- AVIF encode-cost benchmarks: https://webp2png.co/blog/avif-encoding-performance-benchmarks
- JBIG2 (ratios + digit-swap caveat): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/JBIG2
- MRC compression for PDF: https://www.dynamsoft.com/blog/imaging/mrc-compression-pdf/
- Ghostscript PDF optimization: https://ghostscript.com/blog/optimizing-pdfs.html

title: Revisit compose as an in-window docked panel (Gmail-style) — Wayland blocks separate-window dock
description: ng3 smoke #8: operator wanted compose docked bottom-right like Gmail. title: Session-log drain: surface dropped lines when the 500-ring evicts between polls
description: Codex R3 on tuxlink-22l (fix review, 2026-05-20, finding #1). The session-log drain (bootstrap.rs start_drain) polls the durable SessionLogState via snapshot_since(last_seq) every 250ms. If Pat emits >500 stderr lines within one poll interval, the bounded 500-line ring evicts the oldest before the drain reads them, so those lines are SILENTLY skipped (the cursor jumps past them; no double-emit, no crash — just silent loss under extreme burst). This is an inherent property of a bounded ring + a polling consumer; Pat startup is a handful of lines and >2000 lines/sec sustained is extreme, so the v0.0.1 practical risk is low. Polish (v0.1): detect a seq gap (first.seq > last_seq+1 after a poll) and emit a synthetic '[N lines dropped]' marker so loss is VISIBLE rather than silent; or raise the ring cap / poll faster; or add a non-evicting notify queue. Visibility improvement, not a correctness fix.

title: Compose-window capability over-privilege: allow-close/allow-destroy are window-class (can target other windows)
description: Codex P3 (integration-fix re-round, b6331de). compose.json grants core:window:allow-close + allow-destroy. Tauri's window JS sends a caller-supplied label for close/destroy, resolved server-side via manager().get_window(label), so these window-class permissions are NOT scoped to 'self' — a compromised/XSS'd compose window could close/destroy OTHER windows (e.g. main). Normal operation is safe (compose only calls getCurrentWindow().close(); the main-only Rust guard on compose_window_open prevents recursive spawn). allow-destroy is genuinely needed (onCloseRequested uses it when default not prevented), so it can't simply be removed. Bounded surface: close/destroy only — no data exfil or transmission. Fix options: (a) route window-close through a custom Rust command that closes ONLY the calling window and drop the window-class grants from the compose capability; (b) accept the bounded surface as a documented v0.0.1 risk. Hardening; requires an XSS to exploit. Deferred from the integration commit by orchestrator (grouse-esker-juniper, 2026-05-20).
notes: 2026-05-20 gully-sage-heron: PR #98 OPEN. Implemented option (a): compose_close_self command (self-only destroy) + dropped window-class allow-close/allow-destroy from compose.json + routed both close paths through it. Removes the risk rather than accepting it. Gates green. OPERATOR GUI SMOKE is the verification gate (close flows runtime-only). Closes on #98 merge.

title: Harden compose_window_open: validate/encode draft_id before using in window label + route
description: Codex P2 (integration-commit round, 266353a). compose_window_open uses draft_id directly in both the window label (compose-<draftId>) and the route without encoding/validation (compose_window.rs:51). The frontend routing.ts parser handles encoded/missing/trailing-slash/malformed paths safely, and the normal menu path uses safe generated IDs (newDraftId), so this is NOT the normal path — but an IPC caller passing '/', '?', '#', or malformed '%' could create a label/URL that doesn't mount the intended draft. Fix: validate draft_id (charset/length) and/or percent-encode before label+route construction; reject invalid. v0.0.1-safe (generated IDs only), so hardening. Deferred from the integration commit by orchestrator (grouse-esker-juniper, 2026-05-20).
notes: 2026-05-20 gully-sage-heron: PR #99 OPEN (stacked on #98). Added validate_draft_id (pure, TDD'd) + call in compose_window_open. Gates green. Closes on #99 merge (retarget base to feat/v0.0.1 after #98 merges).

title: Wizard MOCKED banner: absent-while-mocked if is_mocked query errors or races a fast mock return
description: Codex Part-97 re-round of b45f592 (P2 visibility, safe-direction failure — no transmission risk). The frontend fails the wizard_test_send_is_mocked query to false (Step3TestSend.tsx:92), so a query error makes the MOCKED banner ABSENT while actually mocked; a fast mocked send can also return before the query resolves. Spec §3.8 wants the banner visible during 'sending' in mock mode so the operator can confirm zero live transmission. Normal local operation shows the banner fine (M1 smoke unaffected). Fix: treat query error as assume-mocked-in-dev / render a pending state, or drive the banner from the same signal the mocked outcome carries. Deferred from M1 by orchestrator (grouse-esker-juniper, 2026-05-20).
notes: 2026-05-20 gully-sage-heron: PR #101 OPEN (stacked on #97). Query is_mocked on mount (static signal) instead of per-sending-entry → fixes the fast-mock-return race; idle-reset dropped; error stays fail-safe (live). Frontend-only. Gates green (vitest 307). Closes on #101 merge.

title: Task 11 live-path failure serialization: WizardError::Other{detail:json} should be structured TestSendOutcome::Failed
description: Codex P2 from Task 11 review (carried, not fixed in b45f592). Live test-send failures (and the timeout path) are serialized as WizardError::Other{detail: <JSON string>} in run_test_send_impl / live_path_outcome_to_wizard_error; the frontend does not parse that JSON and renders 'Unexpected error: {json}'. title: Reading pane: enforce read-side byte cap before buffering full HTTP response (Task 13 Codex finding 3)
description: Codex post-subagent review of Task 13 (bd-tuxlink-y5c) found the 2 MiB cap is enforced inside parse_raw_rfc5322 — AFTER message_read awaits backend.read_message_in and PatClient::read buffers the entire HTTP response via resp.bytes().await (pat_client.rs:166). The UI cannot bypass the parser cap, but a buggy/oversized Pat endpoint can force unbounded memory/network work before the cap trips. Pat is trusted localhost so risk is low; this is v0.1 upstream-cap hardening (stream + early-abort on Content-Length / chunk count). Only exercised once live Pat bootstrap (tuxlink-22l) flows real messages. Deferred from the y5c merge per orchestrator triage (grouse-esker-juniper, 2026-05-20).

title: Tauri webview shows TV-static / GPU artifacts on first launch (Pi 5, Wayland, webkit2gtk-4.1)
description:
Symptom: every time `pnpm tauri dev` (and presumably `pnpm tauri build` AppImage) launches the Tauri window on the dev Pi 5, the window appears as TV-static / uninitialized-GPU-memory graphical artifacts. Any click or window manipulation triggers a repaint and the page content appears normally.

This is an upstream webkit2gtk + Wayland + Mesa V3D (Pi 5 VideoCore VII GPU) class of bug — the WebKit GPU compositor allocates the webview surface before the first frame is painted, so the window shows whatever's in uninitialized GPU memory until an event forces a repaint.

NOT introduced by tuxlink code. Has been present since the Tauri scaffold was added (commit 52d4181, 2026-04-22). Just easier to notice now after Task 7's menu work made the operator stare at the window carefully.

Mitigation env vars to test (least invasive first):
- WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 — disables WebKit's DMA-BUF rendering path, known fix for several Wayland-render bugs
- WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1 — disables GPU compositing entirely, software fallback
- GDK_BACKEND=x11 — falls back to XWayland; nuclear option

If any mitigation works, bake it into:
- The dev experience (env var in package.json scripts or .env.local equivalent)
- The release build (tauri.conf.json's runtime config or AppImage launcher script)

For Task 17 (AppImage packaging, tuxlink-cs7): the release artifact MUST ship with the working mitigation env var pre-set so end users don't see TV-static on their Pi/Linux desktops.

Acceptance:
- Operator verifies one of the env vars eliminates the TV-static symptom on their Pi 5
- Working env var is documented in development.md
- AppImage build (Task 17) bakes the env var into the launcher

Priority P3 (polish — doesn't block functionality; v0.0.1 ships with or without this fix per operator call).

title: Self-generated weather reports from APRS WX + satellite (GOES) ingest
description:
Idea capture (fen-alder-bog, 2026-05-22). Far-future, exploratory — a wild departure from the core 'simple email client' framing, recorded so it isn't lost.

VISION
Ingest APRS weather-station data and combine it with ingested raw satellite imagery/data (GOES or similar) to generate and distribute Tuxlink's OWN weather reports — rather than only relaying others'. Local AI is a candidate for the synthesis/report-generation step.

ARCHITECTURE SKETCH (operator's, verbatim intent)
- Background service requiring AT MINIMUM two dedicated SDRs:
    * SDR #1 — APRS weather-station data ingest.
    * SDR #2 — satellite receive (GOES or similar).
- A dedicated middleware service ingests + parses both data streams.
- Middleware exposes the parsed/combined data to a Tuxlink daemon at a localhost port.

NOTES
- 'It could be done, though.' — operator. Recorded as feasible-but-unscoped; no commitment, no timeline.
- Not yet through office-hours / brainstorming. Promote to a design exploration before any build work.
notes: Hardware dependency: 2+ dedicated SDRs (one APRS WX, one satellite RX). RF receive-only ingest — distinct from the transmit paths gated by RADIO-1. Distribution step (how reports go out) is unspecified and is where Part 97 / RADIO-1 transmission rules would re-enter scope. Local-AI synthesis ties to the existing 'leverage local AI' thread.

value: CORRECTED 2026-05-22: 52.37.245.31 = cms-z.winlink.org itself (my earlier 'stale server.winlink.org rotation IP / Tailscale MagicDNS' theory was WRONG). Probed: cms-z(52.37.245.31): 8772 plaintext OPEN (dev, accepts unregistered tuxlink SID), 8773 FILTERED (NO TLS). server.winlink.org(32.196.219.66/52.206.142.38): 8773 TLS OPEN w/ valid *.winlink.org cert but REJECTS unregistered tuxlink-0.0.1 SID ('Unknown client types... use cms-z'). NET: there is NO working dev-TLS path (cms-z=plaintext-only; prod-TLS needs the tuxlink client SID registered w/ Winlink first per project-cms-rejects-unknown-clients). 8773 IS the correct TLS port. Earlier 52.37.245.31:8773 timeouts were the app correctly dialing cms-z:8773 (no TLS), not a stale server IP.

Winlink B2F already LZHUF-compresses body+attachments losslessly in transit, modem runs MODE 0 (no native compression) — so 'beat .zip' is the wrong frame; re-zipping already-compressed data is a near-no-op. The only lever is reducing SOURCE bytes (resize / lossy re-encode / transcode / format-substitute) before B2F sees them. Binding constraint: recipients run Winlink Express on Windows — only jpeg/gif/png/bmp/tiff/pdf/text/csv/rtf are universally decodable; WebP/AVIF/JXL/HEIC are non-deliverable. Sweet spot for photos = libvips + jpegli (modern-encoder gains, output is still a normal JPEG). On Pi: avoid libaom-AVIF (CPU hog) and guetzli (RAM hog). Full analysis: tuxlink-pxf + dev/scratch/attachment-compression-feasibility.md (local).

Packet B2F end-to-end findings (tuxlink-3wh, 2026-05-22): (1) FBB master/answerer handshake = client handshake + trailing '>' on the DE line (build_master_handshake); the master reads the slave handshake terminating on a peeked 'F' line (its message turn) NOT a '>' prompt (read_slave_handshake) — grounded in wl2k-go fbb/handshake.go. (2) Ax25Stream::read returns Ok(0) for 'no data yet' (defect-J), which BufReader/read_until treat as EOF -> B2F over packet MUST go through BlockingB2fStream (winlink_backend.rs) or it prematurely aborts on RF latency. (3) A localhost TCP relay (full-duplex/lossless) MASKS RF-real bugs (premature-EOF, missing KISS params on answer()) — Codex caught all three; validate against latency + an independent KISS decoder (tuxlink-mbw), not just peer-to-peer.

PROVEN ON-AIR 2026-05-22 (Cameron): BTECH UV-Pro KISS-TNC over Bluetooth = classic RFCOMM/SPP (NOT BLE-GATT). Connected-mode AX.25 WORKS over the UV-Pro on Linux: radio keyed, SABM transmitted, gateway W7MOT-6 answered with DM (two-way SABM-out/DM-back). DM = gateway refused at app layer (likely wrong SSID/target), NOT a transport failure — the link carried it. *** SPP RFCOMM CHANNEL IS DYNAMIC: observed 4 then 5 minutes apart on unit 38:D2:00:01:55:5C (DC6AP's VR-N76 was 1). It rotates per connect/registration — MUST read from SDP each time (sdptool records <MAC> | grep -A1 'SPP Dev' -> the Serial Port 0x1101 RFCOMM channel), NEVER hardcode. Binding a stale/wrong channel -> open connects to nothing -> 'closed' -> first write 'Broken pipe'. This makes a manual 'rfcomm bind <fixed N>' fundamentally unreliable for this radio -> strengthens the deferred in-app RFCOMM-socket transport (reads SDP channel + opens socket at connect time; non-root socket creation already confirmed uid 1000). *** Audio/headset was a RED HERRING: input.conf Disable=Headset did NOT matter, PipeWire did not grab audio; the audio-headset class coexists with SPP (operator: on Windows it's an audio device AND a COM port). ConnectionAttemptFailed at pairing was transient (retry fixed it). Setup: bluetoothctl pair+trust (radio menu Pairing) -> read SPP channel from SDP -> sudo rfcomm bind /dev/rfcomm0 <MAC> <SDP-channel> -> dial promptly (channel can rotate) -> sudo rfcomm release 0. Enable on radio: Menu>General Settings>KISS TNC. OPEN: clean message transfer (got a refusal, not yet a successful B2F) + the double-key/param bug below.
